name: RAGAS (RAG Evaluation Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Exploding Gradients; uses LLM APIs for evaluation (costs apply)

auth.notes: Requires LLM API key (OpenAI by default) for LLM-as-judge metrics

performance.notes: LLM-as-judge evaluation; latency proportional to dataset size and LLM speed

agent_readiness.notes: RAG-specific evaluation metrics using LLM-as-judge. Metrics: faithfulness (hallucination), answer_relevancy, context_recall, context_precision, answer_correctness. evaluate(dataset, metrics=[faithfulness, answer_relevancy]). Supports any LLM via LangChain or custom wrappers. EvaluationDataset from list of dicts. Synthetic test set generation from documents. Integrates with LangChain, LlamaIndex, Haystack. HuggingFace dataset export. Reference-free metrics (no ground truth needed for some). Widely used in RAG evaluation research.

gotchas: LLM-as-judge costs accumulate quickly on large datasets
Metrics require reference answers for some scorers
v0.2 has breaking changes from v0.1
LLM judge variability — results not perfectly reproducible
best_when: Evaluating RAG pipeline quality (faithfulness, relevancy, recall)
Automated regression testing of RAG system changes
Synthetic test set generation from document corpus
avoid_when: Non-RAG LLM evaluation (use deepeval or inspect-ai instead)
Cost-sensitive evaluation at scale (LLM judge expensive)
summary: RAGAS provides RAG-specific evaluation metrics: faithfulness (hallucination detection), answer_relevancy, context_recall/precision, answer_correctness. LLM-as-judge approach. evaluate(dataset, metrics=[...]) with any LLM backend. Synthetic test set generation from documents. Integrates with LangChain, LlamaIndex, Haystack. The standard RAG evaluation library — widely used for comparing chunking, embedding, and retrieval configurations.
